title: SDUI Screen Schema
description: Server-Driven UI schema for NBA Game Detail screens

AccessibilityProperties.description: Server-provided accessibility metadata applied natively per platform

label.description: Human-readable label announced by screen readers. Omit for elements whose text content is self-describing.

role.description: Semantic role override. 'none' suppresses the element's intrinsic role.

hidden.description: When true, element and its descendants are hidden from the accessibility tree (decorative content).

headingLevel.description: Heading level (1-6) for role=heading elements. Maps to aria-level (Web), accessibilityAddTraits .isHeader (iOS), semantics { heading() } (Android).

liveRegion.description: Announces content changes. 'polite' waits for idle; 'assertive' interrupts. Maps to aria-live (Web), accessibilityLiveRegion (Android), .accessibilityAddTraits (iOS).

sortOrder.description: Override default accessibility traversal order. Lower values are visited first. Omit to use natural DOM/view order.

hint.description: Additional context announced after the label. Maps to accessibilityHint (iOS), contentDescription suffix (Android), aria-describedby text (Web).

TextVariant.description: Material3 typography scale plus the NBA-specific `score` variant (monospaced digits for live score / clock rendering).

TextWeight.description: Font weight tokens for atomic Text elements.

ContainerVariant.description: Role-based container presets that carry platform-native surface treatments inline props cannot cleanly express. 'hero' maps to OS-tiered materials (Liquid Glass on iOS 26+, .ultraThinMaterial on iOS 17–25, Material 3 Expressive on Android 15+, Material You on 12–14, CSS surface mixin on web). 'grouped' maps to the platform's inset-grouped list idiom (SwiftUI .insetGrouped / Material ListItem grouping). Each value must clear the variant-discipline inexpressibility bar — do not add values that can be produced by inline background + cornerRadius + shadow + padding. Inline style properties may still be overridden per the variant's override matrix.

ImageVariant.description: Image presentation presets that carry platform-native content mode, corner radius, and clipping inline props cannot cleanly express. 'thumbnail' maps to the platform's cropped media-tile idiom (Coil cross-fade + diskCachePolicy on Android, SwiftUI .clipShape(.rect(cornerRadius:)) with content-mode placeholder reservation on iOS, object-fit: cover + CSS aspect-ratio reserve on web). New values must clear the Rule 19 inexpressibility bar — values that reduce to cornerRadius + aspectRatio + fit do not belong here.

SelectVariant.description: How a Form single-select field is realized by the client. 'dropdown' maps to the platform menu (default). 'chips' is a horizontally-scrollable row of tappable capsules. Applies only when FormField.fieldType == 'select'.

SizingMode.description: Sizing behavior along one axis. 'hug' sizes to content (default). 'fill' stretches to parent available space. 'fixed' uses the explicit width/height value.

ActionTrigger.description: Event that fires the action. Prefer onActivate for primary activation (tap, keyboard Enter/Space, accessibility activate). onTap is a deprecated alias for onActivate.

visibility.description: Fraction of section area that must be visible (0.5 = 50%)

dwellMs.description: Milliseconds section must remain visible before impression fires

ImpressionPolicy.description: Impression tracking policy for analytics actions with onVisible trigger

FailurePolicy.description: Sequence behavior when an action fails. Clients apply per-type defaults when absent: navigate=halt, fireAndForget/dismiss/toast=silent, mutate/refresh=continue.

FailureFeedbackStyle.description: Presentation hint for failure feedback. Clients map to closest platform-native mechanism.

FailureFeedback.description: Optional server-provided error message and presentation style for action failures. Client falls back to generic localized string when absent.

message.description: Localized error message to display on failure

style.description: Presentation hint — clients map to closest platform-native mechanism

targetUri.description: For navigate actions: native deeplink URI

webUrl.description: For navigate actions: web-equivalent URL (first-class, not a fallback)

presentation.description: For navigate actions: how the destination is presented

modalHeight.description: For navigate actions with modal presentation: sheet height

destinations.description: For fireAndForget actions: where to send the beacon

impression.description: For fireAndForget actions with onVisible trigger: impression tracking policy

target.description: For mutate actions: state key to update. For dismiss actions: what to dismiss (modal/overlay/screen). For refresh actions: section ID to refresh (omit for full screen)

operation.description: For mutate actions: operation to perform on the state key

value.description: For mutate actions: the value to apply with the operation

endpoint.description: For refresh actions: target URL (defaults to current screen endpoint if omitted)

paramBindings.description: For refresh actions: map of query param name to screen state key, resolved at action time

message.description: For toast actions: text message to display in the toast

onFailure.description: Sequence behavior when this action fails. Client applies per-type default when absent (navigate=halt, fireAndForget/dismiss/toast=silent, mutate/refresh=continue)

failureFeedback.description: Optional server-provided error message and presentation style. Client falls back to generic localized string when absent

intervalMs.description: For poll type: interval in milliseconds

url.description: For poll/sse type: URL to poll or connect to. If omitted, polls the SDUI endpoint.

channel.description: For sse type: Ably channel name pattern (e.g., '{gameId}:linescore')

dataPath.description: JSONPath to extract section data from response (e.g., '$.game' or '$.sections[0].data')

pauseWhenOffScreen.description: Whether the client should pause this section's refresh when it scrolls out of the viewport. Default true. Set false for critical live sections (e.g., live-score panels) that should refresh continuously.

transform.description: Optional server-declared transform applied by shared client binding infrastructure before writing the target value. liveClockSnapshot normalizes clock payload values into { snapshotSeconds, snapshotAt, isRunning }.

stringKeys.description: Optional map of targetPath to translation key for client-side i18n resolution on bound fields

ColorToken.description: A color value on the wire. Either a literal hex color (#RRGGBB or #RRGGBBAA) or a semantic token reference of the form 'token:<dot.separated.path>' (e.g. 'token:nba.color.primary.50', 'token:nba.label.accent.brand'). Clients resolve token references at render time against their bundled color registry, picking a light or dark value based on the OS color scheme. Unknown tokens log 'token_resolver_missing' and fall back to the caller's default.

LayoutScalar.description: Absolute layout value: raw dp/px integer, or a semantic layout token reference token:<path> (e.g. token:nba.spacing.md, token:nba.radius.lg) resolved per platform.formFactor against bundled spacing/corner/size/typography/shadow registries. Unknown tokens log token_resolver_missing and fall back to 0 (or caller default).

BackgroundGradient.description: Gradient background with ordered color stops

colors.description: Ordered list of color stops (hex or semantic token)

BackgroundImage.description: Image background with optional scale and overlay

overlay.description: Optional overlay applied on top of the image

Background.description: Shared background type — solid color, gradient, or image with overlay

Shadow.description: Shadow effect with CSS/SwiftUI semantics (radius + offset). Compose approximates via elevation. Use 'type' to distinguish drop vs inner shadows.

type.description: Shadow type. 'drop' is an outer shadow (default, backward-compatible). 'inner' is an inset shadow. Platforms without first-class inner shadow support fall back to drop with a diagnostic.

color.description: Shadow color (hex with alpha, or token reference)

Border.description: Outer stroke applied around a container or section.

Badge.description: Z-positioned child element overlaid on a parent (e.g. 'LIVE' pill at bottom-right of a thumbnail, duration label). Named Badge (not Overlay) to avoid collision with the screen-level overlays map.

element.description: The element to render as a badge

alignment.description: Position of the badge within the parent bounds

AtomicOverlay.description: One server-composed overlay layer positioned over an OverlayContainer base element.

alignment.description: Position of the overlay within the base element bounds.

inset.description: Optional edge offsets from the aligned base bounds.

element.description: Atomic element to render in this overlay layer.

PageIndicator.description: Server-declared scroll page indicator presentation for ScrollContainer. Clients own local scroll state only to realize the declared affordance.

style.description: Indicator visualization style. 'dots' renders circular dots; 'dashes' renders horizontal bar segments.

alignment.description: Position of the indicator within the ScrollContainer bounds.

AtomicElement.description: Atomic UI primitive — server-composed building block for the atomic rendering layer

accessibility.description: Server-provided accessibility metadata for this atomic element

gap.description: Gap between flex children (row/column), or grid gap where applicable.

padding.description: Inner space between the element's own background/border and its content.

margin.description: Outer space between the element and its siblings or parent edges. Applied outside the element's background, border, corner radius, and shadow — use this for sibling-to-sibling spacing instead of Spacer siblings when inhomogeneous gaps are needed.

background.description: DEPRECATED — use backgrounds (array) for new payloads. Single background. If both background and backgrounds are present, backgrounds wins.

backgrounds.description: Ordered array of background layers. Index 0 is the bottommost layer (Figma convention); higher indices paint on top. Web renderers must reverse the array when mapping to CSS background shorthand (CSS is top-to-bottom). When absent, falls back to singular background field.

variant.description: Named variant preset. The vocabulary depends on the element's type: TextVariant for Text, ButtonVariant for Button, ContainerVariant for Container, ImageVariant for Image. Renderers parse this string against the primitive's enum and log a diagnostic on unrecognized values.

aspectRatio.description: Aspect ratio: legacy numeric (w/h), or named ratio string for semantic layout.

alt.description: Deprecated: use accessibility.label instead. Retained for backward compatibility; clients prefer accessibility.label when present.

width.description: Fixed width in dp/px or layout token.

height.description: Fixed height in dp/px or layout token.

widthMode.description: Sizing behavior along the width axis. Replaces fillWidth. 'hug' = intrinsic, 'fill' = stretch to parent, 'fixed' = use explicit width value.

heightMode.description: Sizing behavior along the height axis. 'hug' = intrinsic, 'fill' = stretch to parent, 'fixed' = use explicit height value.

fillWidth.description: DEPRECATED — use widthMode: 'fill' instead. Retained for backward compatibility. When true, equivalent to widthMode: 'fill'. If both fillWidth and widthMode are set, widthMode wins.

minWidth.description: Minimum width constraint in dp/px or layout token.

maxWidth.description: Maximum width constraint in dp/px or layout token.

minHeight.description: Minimum height constraint in dp/px or layout token.

maxHeight.description: Maximum height constraint in dp/px or layout token.

layoutWrap.description: When true, enables flex-wrap on a Container. Children that overflow the main axis wrap to the next line. Only meaningful on Container elements.

crossAxisGap.description: Gap between wrapped lines when layoutWrap is true. Falls back to gap when absent. Ignored when layoutWrap is false.

alignSelf.description: Per-child cross-axis alignment override. When set, wins over parent crossAlignment for this child (matches Figma and CSS align-self semantics).

columns.description: DisplayGrid column definitions — display-only, non-interactive, server-ordered

rows.description: DisplayGrid row data — each object maps column keys to pre-formatted display values

section.description: Full section object to render via SectionRouter. Only used when type is SectionSlot.

flex.description: Flex grow factor. When set on a child of a Container, the child claims proportional space along the main axis (like CSS flex or Compose weight). Default 0 (size to content).

breakpoint.description: Responsive breakpoint in dp/px. For Container: below this screen width, direction flips from row to column. Enables responsive layouts without client logic.

cornerRadius.description: Corner radius: dp/px or layout token. Applied to Container (with overflow clip) and Image elements.

cornerRadii.description: Per-corner cornerRadius override. When present, takes precedence over the single-value cornerRadius; any corner key omitted falls back to cornerRadius (or 0 if that is also absent). Used for asymmetric card shapes — e.g. content-rail cards with rounded tops and square bottoms so headline text does not collide with a bottom-corner curve. iOS maps to UnevenRoundedRectangle (iOS 16+); Android to RoundedCornerShape's four-corner constructor; web to borderTopLeftRadius / borderTopRightRadius / borderBottomLeftRadius / borderBottomRightRadius.

topStart.description: Top-leading corner (top-left in LTR, top-right in RTL).

topEnd.description: Top-trailing corner (top-right in LTR, top-left in RTL).

opacity.description: Element opacity (0=transparent, 1=opaque). Enables duration badge overlays and faded states.

shadow.description: DEPRECATED — use shadows (array) for new payloads. Single shadow. If both shadow and shadows are present, shadows wins.

shadows.description: Ordered array of shadow layers. Index 0 is the outermost shadow (Figma convention); higher indices are closer to the element. Maps directly to CSS box-shadow list order. When absent, falls back to singular shadow field.

badge.description: Z-positioned child element (e.g. 'LIVE' pill, duration label) overlaid on this element.

base.description: OverlayContainer base element. Rendered first and sized by its own atomic box model.

overlays.description: OverlayContainer layers rendered over the base element in server order.

textAlign.description: Text alignment within the element. Used for centered headings, right-aligned numeric values.

showIndicators.description: Whether to show scroll indicators on ScrollContainer. Default false for clean carousel presentation.

pageIndicator.description: Optional ScrollContainer page indicator. Clients render it only when declared.

monospacedDigits.description: Use tabular/monospaced digit rendering to prevent layout shift on numeric text changes (scores, clocks).

snapshotSeconds.description: LiveClock: clock value in seconds at the moment captured by snapshotAt. Clients interpolate from this anchor while isRunning == true. Required when type == 'LiveClock'.

snapshotAt.description: LiveClock: wall-clock instant (ISO-8601) at which snapshotSeconds was captured. Clients compute elapsed = now - snapshotAt and derive the displayed value. Required when type == 'LiveClock'.

isRunning.description: LiveClock: whether the clock is actively ticking. When true, clients run a local tick loop at their platform-native refresh cadence (~10Hz) and update the displayed value. When false, clients render snapshotSeconds verbatim.

tickDirection.description: LiveClock tick direction. 'down' decrements from snapshotSeconds toward stopAtSeconds (default 0); 'up' increments from snapshotSeconds with no upper bound unless stopAtSeconds is set.

stopAtSeconds.description: LiveClock: optional clamp. For direction 'down', clock holds at this value once reached. For direction 'up', clock holds once reached. Omit to disable the clamp.

format.description: LiveClock display format. Clients realize using their platform's tabular-numerals typography (equivalent to TextVariant.score).

bindRef.description: Dot-path into the enclosing AtomicComposite's `data.content` object. When set, renderers resolve the leaf's canonical live field from `data.content[bindRef]` at render time and fall back to the inline value when the path is absent. Canonical field per type: Text → content, Button → label, Image → src, LiveClock → an object with {snapshotSeconds, snapshotAt, isRunning}. Placing the binding identifier on the consuming node (rather than in a centrally-declared path-into-tree) lets composers reshape the ui tree without breaking real-time updates; data-bindings on the section envelope continue to write into `content.*`.

AtomicCompositeData.description: Data payload for AtomicComposite sections — ui contains rendering instructions, content carries domain data

ui.description: Root node of the atomic element tree — the rendering instructions

content.description: Optional domain data (strings, URLs, flags) to populate the ui tree. Reserved for future data-binding support.

TabGroupData.description: Tabbed navigation with dynamic content sections per tab

Subsection.description: Nested interaction target within a section (e.g., tappable team area inside a scoreboard)

BoxscoreColumnDefinition.description: Defines a single column in the boxscore table

key.description: Property key on each player's stats object that supplies this column's value

label.description: Column header text displayed to the user

sortable.description: Whether this column supports client-side sorting

highlighted.description: Whether this column should be visually emphasised (e.g., bold)

BoxscorePlayerStatistics.description: Key-value map of stat abbreviation to value for one player row

BoxscorePlayerRow.description: One player row inside a boxscore table

starter.description: Whether this player was in the starting lineup

BoxscoreTableData.description: Typed tabular data for an NBA-style boxscore (one per team)

columns.description: Ordered list of column definitions; clients render left-to-right

players.description: Player rows ordered by server (starters first, then bench)

teamTotals.description: Aggregate row shown at the bottom of the table

sortStateKey.description: Screen-state key holding the current sort column key

sortDirectionStateKey.description: Screen-state key holding the current sort direction (asc/desc)

emptyMessage.description: Text shown when no player rows are available

FormOption.description: One selectable option within a select/radio/checkbox form field

FormField.description: One input field inside a form section

fieldType.description: Input type; clients map to platform-native controls

variant.description: How to realize the control. Applies only when fieldType == 'select'. Missing value is treated as 'dropdown' at render time.

stateKey.description: Screen-state key that holds this field's current value

options.description: For select/radio/checkbox field types: the available choices

validationPattern.description: Optional regex pattern for client-side validation

validationMessage.description: Message to show when validation fails

FormData.description: Server-driven form section with typed fields bound to screen state

submitAction.description: Action fired when the form is submitted

subsections.description: Nested interaction targets within the section

contentSourceId.description: Origin identifier for the content backing this section (e.g. 'cms:article-42', 'stats-api:leaders-2025'). Carried through to analytics for two-tier attribution.

accessibility.description: Section-level accessibility metadata (landmark role, live region, heading)

stringTable.description: Section-level map of translation key to localized string. Used by DataBindingResolver to resolve stringKeys on real-time updates.

SectionSurface.description: Server-driven surface spec applied by the client's SectionRouter to every semantic section — the visual wrapper beneath the section's content. Mirrors the inline-chrome vocabulary on AtomicContainer so semantic sections have schema parity with composed sections. Every client's shared SectionContainer wrapper reads these fields; semantic-section renderers do not set outer padding, margin, corner radius, shadow, border, or background themselves. The sibling `data` field carries content (including the atomic UI tree); `surface` carries the frame that sits beneath it.

margin.description: Outer margin (space between the surface and its siblings / screen edge).

padding.description: Inner padding (space between the surface edge and the content it wraps).

cornerRadius.description: Corner radius: dp/px or layout token, applied to the surface (with overflow clip).

shadow.description: Drop shadow applied to the surface.

border.description: Outer stroke applied around the surface.

SectionLayoutHints.description: Optional layout hints for section placement. Clients apply best-effort; unknown hints are ignored.

dividerAbove.description: Render a divider line above this section

dividerBelow.description: Render a divider line below this section

priority.description: Rendering priority hint — clients may use for lazy loading or viewport priority

SectionStates.description: Server-declared loading and error presentation for a section. Clients render these states when applicable.

skeleton.description: Which loading skeleton style to use

minHeightDp.description: Minimum height to reserve during loading (prevents layout shift)

error.description: Server-declared error presentation for this section.

ErrorState.description: Server-declared error-state shape rendered by section error boundaries. Named `ErrorState` (not `Error`) so the generated client type does not shadow each runtime's native error protocol (e.g. `Swift.Error`).

message.description: Error message to display (e.g., 'Unable to load scores')

retryAction.description: Optional action to trigger on retry tap (typically a refresh action)

retryLabel.description: Button label for the retry CTA (e.g. 'Try Again', 'Reload'). Clients use 'Retry' as a neutral default when omitted.

hideOnError.description: If true, collapse the section entirely on error instead of showing error UI

LeadersTableData.description: Sortable, paginated table of season statistical leaders (league-wide)

subtitle.description: Secondary text, e.g. '2025-26 Regular Season – Per Game'

columns.description: Ordered column definitions; clients render left-to-right

players.description: Player rows, pre-sorted by the server

totalRows.description: Total number of rows available server-side (for pagination display)

sortColumn.description: Key of the column the table is currently sorted by

emptyMessage.description: Text shown when no player rows are available

LeadersPlayerRow.description: One ranked player row in a season leaders table

stats.description: Stat values keyed by column key (gp, min, pts, reb, ast, etc.)

SubscribeBannerData.description: Inline subscription upsell banner. Reserved SDK integration point: the banner's visible chrome is entirely server-composed via `ui` until the platform IAP SDK (StoreKit / Play Billing) lands and starts mounting the purchase flow on CTA tap. `tiers` carries the IAP product identifiers the SDK will later bind to; `ctaAction` is the (pre-SDK) fallback action.

ui.description: Atomic tree describing the banner's full visible surface. Renderer walks this tree exactly as an AtomicComposite would; no client-side chrome defaults are permitted. See AGENTS.md §15.1.

ctaAction.description: Top-level fallback action invoked when the IAP SDK is not mounted (today, always).

tiers.description: IAP product identifiers + server-emitted prices. Consumed by the IAP SDK when it lands; not used by the renderer, which reads the visible price copy out of `ui`.

SubscribeHeroData.description: Full-screen subscription upsell hero. Reserved SDK integration point — same contract as SubscribeBannerData: `ui` carries the full visible composition; `tiers` carries IAP product identifiers the SDK will bind to post-landing.

ui.description: Atomic tree describing the hero's full visible surface — logo, title, subtitle, feature list, tier cards, CTAs. Renderer walks this tree exactly as an AtomicComposite would.

tiers.description: IAP product identifiers + server-emitted prices. Consumed by the IAP SDK when it lands; not used by the renderer.

name.description: Tier name, e.g. 'League Pass', 'League Pass Premium'

AdSlotData.description: Ad placement primitive — carries placement semantics while delegating auction/targeting to ad-platform SDKs (see ADR-007)

adUnitPath.description: Ad unit path used by the ad SDK

sizes.description: Accepted creative sizes as [width, height] pairs

targeting.description: Key-value targeting hints passed to ad SDK

collapseOnEmpty.description: Whether to collapse the slot when no fill is returned

label.description: Disclosure label displayed above/below the ad

placeholder.description: Visual treatment for the reserved rectangle before the ad SDK mounts (and after misses when collapseOnEmpty is false). Shares dimensions with sizes[0] — never carries its own width/height. Required so the stub renderer has no client-side chrome defaults.

backgroundColor.description: Fill color for the empty rectangle.

text.description: Caption rendered inside the empty rectangle (e.g. 'Advertisement').

VideoPlayerData.description: Video player section — reserved SDK integration point for DRM / HLS / ad insertion. `playerType`, `contentId`, `autoplay`, and `capabilities` are SDK inputs (the video SDK reads them and mounts); `ui` carries the pre-SDK placeholder composition that renders before the SDK is integrated and will serve as the loading/error placeholder afterwards.

playerType.description: Discriminator for SDK player variant. Client passes contentId to the matching SDK method.

contentId.description: Content identifier — interpreted by playerType (gameId for game, mediaId for vod, eventId for event, streamUrl for stream). Single field avoids mutually exclusive optional IDs.

capabilities.description: Platform capabilities the player should enable. Server includes only capabilities relevant to the requesting platform (via X-Platform header).

ui.description: Atomic tree describing the pre-SDK placeholder surface (e.g. play-glyph column framed at the player's aspectRatio). Renderer walks this tree exactly as an AtomicComposite would; no client-side chrome defaults are permitted. Once the video SDK lands this tree becomes the loading/error placeholder the SDK overlays.

State.description: Screen-level state map for interactive patterns

parentUri.description: URI the back button should navigate to.  Clients always show a back button; this field tells them the target.  Omit for root screens (e.g. scoreboard).

actions.description: Screen-level actions (e.g. analytics beacons, lifecycle hooks)

overlays.description: Named overlay sections the client shows when a trigger condition arises. Keys are developer-defined state names (e.g. 'couchRightsWarning'). Values are server-composed sections (typically AtomicComposite). Client controls trigger timing and presentation style; server controls display content.

variants.description: Server-exposed A/B / experiment variants available for this screen. Clients read `options` to render a variant picker (dev UI, QA tooling) and pass the selected id back to the server on subsequent requests. Omit for screens without active experiments.

ExperimentVariants.description: Wrapper for the set of A/B variants the server is willing to serve for this screen. Lets clients expose variant selection without hardcoding experiment ids or option vocabularies.

experimentId.description: Stable identifier for the experiment (e.g. `game_detail_variant`). Clients echo this key back to the server as part of the experiments map on subsequent requests.

options.description: Ordered list of variants the client may choose from.

label.description: Human-readable label rendered in variant pickers.

description.description: Optional longer description shown alongside the label.
